summary: Bind Windows signed installed admission to live share-denied filesystem objects and block child creation until the suspended native adapter exists.
rationale: Issue #368 is the first of two deliberately split Windows launch-binding slices. It adds one Windows-only native owner for local-NTFS path components, manifest, detached signature, and sidecar executable handles; signed admission reads and hashes those same open objects. The existing live installed-slot lease owns the native handles. Windows spawn then fails closed before Popen until #369 provides suspended CreateProcessW and child-image identity. macOS behavior and production composition remain unchanged.
verification: uv run ruff check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_pr_governance.py

uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_windows_installed_binding.py tests/test_installed_release.py tests/test_installed_release_admission.py tests/test_installed_slot_lease.py tests/test_owned_sidecar_process.py -q
uv run --group dev ruff check src tests experiments tools
uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run --group dev python tools/check_privacy_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_agent_safety_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_ai_bad_smells.py --base origin/main
deletion_targets: Windows path-only signed admission whose inspected descriptors close before child creation
Windows subprocess.Popen fallback before suspended child-image binding exists
caller-fabricated or serialized Windows native handle authority
risks: This slice intentionally makes Windows child creation unavailable until #369; production caller count is already zero.
GitHub Windows Server 2025 proves local-NTFS API semantics but not Windows 10/11 installer, Defender, filter-driver, or enterprise-policy behavior.
Authenticode, complete packaged payload closure, and workflow session authority remain later independent gates.
